Multi-omics analysis reveals PIAS family gene dysregulation as a driver of head and neck squamous cell carcinoma (HNSC) progression and therapeutic vulnerability

evidence_entities.text: RT-qPCR demonstrated significant up-regulation of PIAS1, PIAS2, PIAS3, and PIAS4 in HNSC cell lines
Promoter methylation analysis revealed a negative correlation, suggesting hypomethylation as a potential mechanism for their overexpression
Survival analysis implicated elevated PIAS expression in poorer overall survival of HNSC patients
Functional assays demonstrated that PIAS knockdown inhibited HNSC cell proliferation, migration, and invasion
identification of Cyclocreatine and Vorinostat as potential inhibitors through drug acquisition and molecular docking
